question:
Vì sao chiến lược RAG trong đoạn này giúp giảm hallucination: # Attention (machine learning)

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_(machine_learning)

In machine learning,?
answer: Chiến lược RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) giúp giảm hallucination trong mô hình ngôn ngữ bằng cách kết hợp thông tin từ nguồn dữ liệu bên ngoài với khả năng sinh ngôn ngữ của mô hình. Điều này cho phép mô hình truy xuất thông tin chính xác và đáng tin cậy hơn, từ đó giảm thiểu khả năng tạo ra thông tin sai lệch hoặc không chính xác. Bằng cách sử dụng các trọng số "mềm" trong quá trình chú ý, mô hình có thể xác định và tập trung vào các phần quan trọng của dữ liệu, cải thiện độ chính xác của đầu ra.
contexts:
["# Attention (machine learning)

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_(machine_learning)

In machine learning, attention is a method that determines the importance of each component in a sequence relative to the other components in that sequence. In natural language processing, importance is represented by "soft" weights assigned to each word in a sentence. More generally, attention encodes vectors called token embeddings across a fixed-width sequence that can range from tens to millions of tokens in size.
Unlike "hard" weights, which are computed during the backwards training pass, "soft" weights exist only in the forward pass and therefore change with every step of the input. Ear", "# Quantum machine learning

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning

Quantum machine learning (QML) is the study of quantum algorithms for machine learning. It often refers to quantum algorithms for machine learning tasks which analyze classical data, sometimes called quantum-enhanced machine learning.
QML algorithms use qubits and quantum operations to try to improve the space and time complexity of classical machine learning algorithms. Hybrid QML methods involve both classical and quantum processing, where computationally difficult subroutines are outsourced to a quantum device. These routines can be more complex in nature and executed faster on a quantum computer. ", "# Neural network (machine learning)

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neural_network_(machine_learning)

In machine learning, a neural network (NN) or neural net, also known as an artificial neural network (ANN), is a computational model inspired by the structure and functions of biological neural networks.
A neural network consists of connected units or nodes called artificial neurons, which loosely model the neurons in the brain. Artificial neuron models that mimic biological neurons more closely have also been recently investigated and shown to significantly improve performance. question:
Tóm tắt ý chính của đoạn sau: # Attention (machine learning)

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_(machine_learning)

In machine learning,?
answer: In machine learning, attention is a technique that assesses the significance of each element in a sequence relative to others, particularly in natural language processing. It assigns "soft" weights to words in a sentence, which vary with each input step, as opposed to "hard" weights that are fixed during training. Attention encodes token embeddings across sequences that can range from tens to millions of tokens.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_(machine_learning)

In machine learning,?
answer: In machine learning, attention is a technique that assesses the significance of each element in a sequence relative to others, particularly in natural language processing. It assigns "soft" weights to words in a sentence, which vary with each input step, as opposed to "hard" weights that are fixed during training. Attention encodes token embeddings across sequences that can range from tens to millions of tokens.

Quantum machine learning (QM?
answer: Quantum machine learning (QML) is the exploration of quantum algorithms designed for machine learning tasks, often enhancing classical data analysis. QML utilizes qubits and quantum operations to improve the efficiency of classical algorithms. Hybrid methods combine classical and quantum processing, allowing complex tasks to be executed more rapidly on quantum devices.

Quantum machine learning (QML) is the study of quantum algorithms for machine learning. It often refers to quantum algorithms for machine learning tasks which analyze classical data, sometimes called quantum-enhanced machine learning.
QML algorithms use qubits and quantum operations to try to improve the space and time complexity of classical machine learning algorithms. Hybrid QML methods involve both classical and quantum processing, where computationally difficult subroutines are outsourced to a quantum device. Hybrid QML methods involve both classical and quantum processing, where computationally difficult subroutines are outsourced to a quantum device. These routines can be more complex in nature and executed faster on a quantum computer. ", "# Attention (machine learning)

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_(machine_learning)

In machine learning, attention is a method that determines the importance of each component in a sequence relative to the other components in that sequence. In natural language processing, importance is represented by "soft" weights assigned to each word in a sentence. More generally, attention encodes vectors called token embeddings across a fixed-width sequence that can range from tens to millions of tokens in size.
Unlike "hard" weights, which are computed during the backwards training pass, "soft" weights exist only in the forward pass and therefore change with every step of the input. Ear"]

Artificial gen?
answer: Artificial general intelligence (AGI) is a theoretical form of AI that can perform any cognitive task at or above human levels. It differs from artificial narrow intelligence (ANI), which is limited to specific tasks. AGI can generalize knowledge, transfer skills, and solve new problems without needing specific reprogramming. The development of AGI is a goal for many technology companies.
